title: Fresh American Cucumber Flatbread
description: A malty, flaky post-workout American dish featuring cucumber, this flatbread is perfect for a light snack, and it's dairy-free-friendly. It is served on a talavera plate.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, malted barley flour, salt, and sugar.
Add the cold vegan butter to the dry ingredients and use a pastry blender to work it into the mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add the ice water to the mixture, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together in a ball.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and roll it out into a large rectangle.
Fold the dough in half lengthwise, then fold it in half again, rotating the dough 90 degrees after each fold.
Roll the dough out into a large rectangle again, then repeat the folding process two more times.
After the final fold, roll the dough out into a large rectangle and place it on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Arrange the sliced cucumber on one half of the dough, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Sprinkle the chopped fresh dill over the cucumber.
Fold the other half of the dough over the filling and press the edges to seal.
Bake the flatbread in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until it is golden brown.
